name: Carnival of Recife and Olinda
traditions: Galo da Madrugada (world's largest carnival block); frevo dancing with colorful umbrellas; maracatu drumming; giant puppets in Olinda.
historicalBackground: Recife and Olinda's carnival preserves distinct Northeast Brazilian traditions including frevo (invented in Recife around 1900) and maracatu of African origin.
description: Brazil's most culturally rich carnival, blending frevo's acrobatic dancing with maracatu drumming and Olinda's giant puppet parades.
